steps.title: Identify similar triangles
steps.content: The angle marks indicate angle QWX = angle QYZ. Combined with angle WQX = angle YQZ (vertical angles), triangle WQX is similar to triangle YQZ by AA similarity.

steps.title: Apply the proportion to find YZ
steps.content:
For similar triangles, corresponding sides are proportional:

$$\frac{WQ}{YQ} = \frac{WX}{YZ}$$

$$\frac{90}{27} = \frac{60}{YZ}$$

Cross multiply:

$$90 \cdot YZ = 1620$$

$$YZ = 18$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
